free_text: Investigating the nuclear fusion and nucleon transfer
channels in the 197Au + 6He reaction at 6He energies
the U400M cyclotron of the FLNR, JINR
A beam of accelerated 6He ions with an energy of up to
about 20 MeV/A was used. The beam profile and intensity
were measured with a specially designed multi-wire
proportional diagnostic chamber situated immediately in
Au foils of 4 to 12 mum thick were used. Al foil
absorbers were placed between the gold targets.
Two series of experiments were performed using
the activation method to measure the target's activity.
a delta-E-E telescope consisting of a
delta-E semiconductor, a Si detector and a CsI crystal
that was used as an E-detector.
The gamma-activity induced in the targets was
measured on a HPGe detector with 20% efficiency
relative to the NaI and HWHM of 1.8 keV for a gamma-ray
production cross-sections were due to errors in
determining the peak areas in the gamma spectra with
